LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT MOSY DOROODIAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT DEMONSTRATES THAT PLAINTIFF'S CAUSES OF ACTION ARISE FROM PETITIONING ACTIVITY IN CONNECTION WITH DEFENDANT'S PRODUCTION OF EVIDENCE AND TESTIMONY AT JUDICIAL PROCEEDINGS. (SEE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT PAGE 9, LINE 28 - P. 10, LINE 3; PAGE 12, LINES 1-6; P. 13, LINES 4-15.) PLAINTIFF CONTENDS THAT DEFENDANT MADE ORAL AND WRITTEN STATEMENTS IN CONNECTION WITH A MARITAL SETTLEMENT, INCLUDING PROVIDING THESE STATEMENTS AND DOCUMENTS TO THE COURT. THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR "BREACH OF OBLIGATION THROUGH INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION AND DECEIT, ETC." INCORPORATES ALL OF THE FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS FROM THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, AND INCLUDES ALLEGATIONS THAT DEFENDANT "PRODUCED HIS FRAUDULENT HANDIWORK TO THE COURT AT THE APRIL 9, 2009 HEARING IN ORDER TO DECEIVE THE COURT" ABOUT WRITTEN AGREEMENTS BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT REGARDING THEIR MARITAL SETTLEMENT THAT ARE ATTACHED TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. PAGES 19-24 OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT STATE THAT THEY ARE "OFFERED FOR THEIR HISTORICAL BACKGROUND ONLY, AS THEY ARE TIME-BARRED AND INADMISSIBLE AS FRAUD CLAIMS OF THEIR OWN," AND THUS ARE NOT ADDRESSED BY THE COURT. THE BURDEN SHIFTED TO PLAINTIFF TO SHOW A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON HER CLAIMS, WHICH SHE HAS FAILED TO DO. CIVIL CODE SECTION 47(B) PROVIDES AN ABSOLUTE PRIVILEGE FOR PUBLICATION MADE IN A JUDICIAL PROCEEDING. FURTHER, PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATIONS DO NOT SHOW A LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING. HER DECLARATION DOES NOT DEMONSTRATE "FRAUD." FURTHER, PLAINTIFF ADMITS THAT SHE WAS SUFFERING FROM THE INFLUENCE OF PSYCHOLOGICAL MALADIES AND WAS UNDER THE INFLUENCE OF POWERFUL MEDICATIONS AT THE TIME. (SUPPLEMENT DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF AMENDED OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO STRIKE, FILED NOV. 16, 2012, AT PAGE 13.) THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT'S
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLT FILED BY DEFENDANT DOROODIAN, MOSY
MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-06-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-25-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kenyette C. Jones, Esq. of the Law Offices of Kenyette C. Jones, Tel. 415.788.3600, for defendant and moving party;
Cherie Safapou, Tel. 415.686.3807, plaintiff appearing on her behalf.

RULING: The matter was argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MOSY DOROODIAN's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - The anti-SLAPP motion to strike is granted. Defendant demonstrates that plaintiff's causes of action arise from petitioning activity in connection with Defendant's production of evidence and testimony at judicial proceedings. (See First Amended Complaint page 9, line 28 - p. 10, line 3; page 12, lines 1-6; p. 13, lines 4-15.) Plaintiff contends that defendant made oral and written statements in connection with a marital settlement, including providing these statements and documents to the court. The second cause of action for "breach of obligation through intentional misrepresentation and deceit, etc." incorporates all of the factual allegations from the first cause of action, and includes allegations that Defendant "produced his fraudulent handiwork to the Court at the April 9, 2009 hearing in order to deceive the Court" about written agreements between Plaintiff and Defendant regarding their marital settlement that are attached to the Amended Complaint. Pages 19-24 of the Amended Complaint state that they are "offered for their historical background only, as they are time-barred and inadmissible as fraud claims of their own," and thus are not addressed by the Court. The burden shifted to Plaintiff to show a probability of prevailing on her claims, which she has failed to do. Civil Code section 47(b) provides an absolute privilege for publication made in a judicial proceeding. Further, plaintiff's declarations do not show a likelihood of prevailing. Her declaration does not demonstrate "fraud." Further, Plaintiff admits that she was suffering from the influence of psychological maladies and was under the influence of powerful medications at the time. (Supplement Declaration In Support of Amended Opposition to Motion to Strike, filed Nov. 16, 2012, at page 13.) The Court grants Defendant's request for judicial notice. Defendant may make a motion for fees per CCP 425.16(c). Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
